source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
One of the cube faces is cut along its diagonals (see the fig.). Which two of the following nets are impossible?
<image1>
<image2>
extraction_notes: Answer field is option letter D; mapped to option text.
Question contains <image1> and <image2> placeholders, but record provides a single image path.

issues: Question text references two image placeholders (<image1>, <image2>), but only one image path is provided.

image_dependency: task asks about cube nets and a face cut along diagonals, which depends on spatial/visual structure not fully specified in text
multi_step: must evaluate multiple candidate nets and determine which are impossible
requires 3D-to-2D cube net reasoning plus tracking the diagonal cuts on one face
multiple-choice structure with five options suggests comparing several cases rather than a single direct lookup
verifiability: answer_type=short_text and answer_text='3 and 5' is explicit
choices are provided, and the answer maps cleanly to option D
the correctness of each net can in principle be checked by geometric folding/consistency rules

risk_flags: Question text references two image placeholders (<image1>, <image2>), but only one image path is provided.
Single-image asset mismatch may reduce confidence in image-grounded verification.

decision_reasons: Question text, one image asset, and an explicit short-text answer are present, so the sample still retains recoverable core semantics.
However, the question references two image placeholders while only one image path is provided, creating an asset mismatch.
Because image dependency is very high, the missing/misaligned visual asset lowers confidence in reliable image-grounded verification.
Following high-recall collection policy, this is better kept for follow-up rather than rejected.
